_doc: BESPOKE creative guidance (UQ-1), hand-authored, NOT generated. TWO dimensions merged into each cell's GenAI prompt + QA gate: (1) `eras` keyed `t<tier>.<class>` (era × class — arc/tone/focus/rhythm/scannability/avoid + qaLookFor/qaReject), and (2) `waves` keyed by wave id (each people's real history/motivations/trades/obstacles + braidAffinity = who they plausibly cross & why). Wave history drives BOTH act uniqueness AND genuine intersections (WV-2 bias + borrowed crossings). This is where uniqueness lives — edit the briefs to steer the corpus, regenerate to realize. qaLookFor/qaReject feed the QA pass per era.

t0.poor.arc: ARRIVAL into nothing — steerage to a tenement; the generation that simply gets a foothold. Holding/endurance: survival IS the achievement.
t0.poor.tone: raw, close, bodily — hunger, cold, the smell of too many people; dignity scraped from almost nothing.
t0.poor.focus: the first foothold: a job, a room, a name said in a new mouth. The cost of being owed nothing.
t0.poor.rhythm: short, breath-held sentences amid the press; exhaustion sets the pace.
t0.poor.scannability: lead each scene with a SHORT punchy line, then one fuller paragraph; whitespace between beats.
t0.poor.avoid: triumph, grand vistas, fast money. This generation survives, it does not rise.
t0.poor.qaLookFor: bodily sensory detail; the specific labor + lodging of THIS people; small dignity under grinding need.
t0.poor.qaReject: any re-statement of when/where ('you arrive in 1885', 'as an immigrant'); instant wealth or comfort; generic poverty that could be any people.

t0.middle.arc: ARRIVAL with a little — a trade, a small stake, a letter of introduction. Cautious rise: a toehold above steerage.
t0.middle.tone: wary optimism; relief at not being lowest, fear of slipping back.
t0.middle.focus: leveraging the small advantage — a skill, a cousin here, a few saved dollars — into a first real position.
t0.middle.scannability: open on a concrete object (tools, stake, ledger); break paragraphs at each choice.
t0.middle.avoid: destitution clichés — this line arrived with SOMETHING; also no instant success.
t0.middle.qaLookFor: the specific trade/skill of THIS people; the network (landsman, parish, clan) that gives the toehold.
t0.middle.qaReject: steerage-destitution framing (that's t0.poor); when/where re-statement; success with no effort.

t1.poor.arc: DIGGING IN — the household takes root in the ward. Holding: slow, accreting, the long toil.
t1.poor.tone: weary persistence; small joys (a wedding, a wage) against grinding labor.
t1.poor.focus: establishing a household + a place in the local economy from the bottom.
t1.poor.rhythm: slow, atmospheric, seasons passing; more white space than incident.
t1.poor.scannability: let scenes breathe — short stanza-like paragraphs; the reader scans the years, lingers on the one that turns.
t1.poor.avoid: melodrama; this is endurance, not spectacle.
t1.poor.qaLookFor: the texture of THIS people's ward (its church/temple, food, mutual-aid); generational time passing.
t1.poor.qaReject: sudden upward mobility; spectacle; a ward that could be any group's.

t1.middle.arc: THE FIRST CLIMB — a trade becomes a small business; standing in the neighborhood. Rise with a real fork.
t1.middle.tone: ambition warming; the pull between consolidating and reaching.
t1.middle.focus: a stake in the local economy — a shop, a contract, a name vendors know.
t1.middle.scannability: front-load each scene's stakes in a crisp opening line; trim paragraphs.
t1.middle.avoid: staying small forever; give a genuine step up.
t1.middle.qaLookFor: the specific business THIS people would build; the community ties that fund/staff it.
t1.middle.qaReject: staying static; a business with no cultural specificity.

t2.poor.arc: THE LONG SHOT — a poor line reaching for the middle against the odds. Reinvention: stuck, then a hinge.
t2.poor.tone: hungry, scrappy, a chip on the shoulder; the system is not built for them.
t2.poor.focus: the pivot — an idea, a risk, a pried-open door — that could break the ceiling or the family.
t2.poor.rhythm: tense, coiled; slow build to a sharp turn.
t2.poor.scannability: build pressure in short lines; the hinge scene gets ONE held, fuller paragraph.
t2.poor.avoid: easy success; the long shot must cost.
t2.poor.qaLookFor: the SPECIFIC barrier THIS people faced (a law, a bias, a closed trade) and the scrappy way around it.
t2.poor.qaReject: frictionless breakthrough; a barrier with no historical specificity.

t2.middle.arc: AMBITION VS LOYALTY — the climb out of the working class strains the old bonds. Rise shadowed by what's left behind.
t2.middle.tone: bittersweet of rising; new rooms, old guilt.
t2.middle.focus: the family's rise into comfort and what it asks them to leave or betray.
t2.middle.rhythm: smooth then snagging — momentum interrupted by a loyalty it tests.
t2.middle.scannability: alternate a clipped advancement beat with a fuller cost beat; clear breaks.
t2.middle.avoid: frictionless ascent; the rise must have a moral price.
t2.middle.qaLookFor: the specific old-world/community bond the rise strains; an assimilation cost particular to THIS people.
t2.middle.qaReject: rising with no cost; generic 'family drama' untethered from the people's history.

t3.poor.arc: A NAME, AGAINST TYPE — a once-poor line that now carries weight. Rivalry: defined by who they're measured against.
t3.poor.tone: proud, embattled; never quite accepted by the established.
t3.poor.focus: influence earned the hard way, and the rivals/gatekeepers crossed to get here.
t3.poor.rhythm: confrontational; scenes pivot on encounters with other lines.
t3.poor.scannability: stage each rival meeting as a sharp exchange — short lines, then the consequence.
t3.poor.avoid: smooth belonging; this name is contested.
t3.poor.qaLookFor: the specific establishment that resisted THIS people; an earned, contested authority.
t3.poor.qaReject: easy acceptance; rivals with no historical grounding.

t3.middle.arc: POWER AND ITS COSTS — a comfortable line reaching for real influence. Reinvention/rise with a moral fork.
t3.middle.tone: assured, then unsettled; the air thinner the higher they go.
t3.middle.focus: influence over the era's turns — and what it does to the people who wield it.
t3.middle.rhythm: controlled, deliberate; the major turn lands with weight.
t3.middle.scannability: keep the worldly machinery scannable — stake, move, fallout in distinct beats.
t3.middle.qaLookFor: the specific institution/era-turn THIS people would influence; a real moral price.
t3.middle.qaReject: consequence-free power; influence with no period specificity.

t4.poor.arc: THE IMPROBABLE EMPIRE — a line that climbed from nothing now shapes the century. Windfall-then-reckoning.
t4.poor.tone: vast but vertiginous; the scale dwarfs the scrappy origin still in their bones.
t4.poor.focus: scale and consequence — decisions that move systems, made by people who remember the tenement.
t4.poor.rhythm: sweeping passages cut by sudden intimate ones (the origin surfacing).
t4.poor.scannability: big-canvas scenes still scan — one vivid image per beat, not dense exposition.
t4.poor.avoid: forgetting where they came from; let the origin haunt the height.
t4.poor.qaLookFor: the origin memory surfacing at scale; system-moving decisions with a human root.
t4.poor.qaReject: amnesiac grandeur; dense exposition that doesn't scan.

t4.middle.arc: THE WORLD PLAYER — an established house at global scale. Rivalry/collapse: contenders at the summit.
t4.middle.tone: imperial, cold at the edges; the human shrinking against the machine.
t4.middle.focus: shaping the globe's systems — and the rival houses contending for the same height.
t4.middle.rhythm: grand, then brittle; collapse risk under the reach.
t4.middle.scannability: summit scenes legible — the contest, the maneuver, the cost, each its own clean beat.
t4.middle.qaLookFor: a real contest with a peer house; brittleness under the scale.

t5.poor.arc: THE LONGEST REACH — a line that began in steerage now reaches the stars. Reinvention: the ultimate transformation.
t5.poor.tone: luminous, awed, the origin almost mythic; humanity's frontier through one family's eyes.
t5.poor.focus: the line's mark on the cosmos — carrying a tenement memory into the colonized stars.
t5.poor.rhythm: expansive, open-fifth; long luminous passages, the past a quiet undertone.
t5.poor.scannability: cosmic scale stays readable — one clear wonder per beat; resist dense hard-SF blocks.
t5.poor.avoid: sterile sci-fi; keep the human, immigrant thread.
t5.poor.qaLookFor: the immigrant origin echoing at the cosmic scale; awe grounded in one family.
t5.poor.qaReject: sterile/technical hard-SF that drops the human + immigrant thread; jargon walls.

t5.middle.arc: THE DYNASTY AMONG STARS — an arrived house extending beyond Earth. Holding/ascension: legacy at the frontier.
t5.middle.tone: stately, far-seeing; the weight of a long line at its furthest point.
t5.middle.focus: the dynasty's foothold beyond Earth — consolidation and legacy at cosmic scale.
t5.middle.rhythm: measured, monumental; the close carries the whole line's arc.
t5.middle.scannability: frontier grandeur in scannable beats — image, stake, choice, each distinct.
t5.middle.avoid: anticlimax; the final reach must feel earned by six generations.
t5.middle.qaLookFor: the six-generation arc landing in the close; legacy with weight.
t5.middle.qaReject: anticlimax; cosmic setting with no through-line to the family's history.

ireland.history: Famine-driven Catholic emigration; landed destitute into anti-Irish, anti-Catholic nativism (No Irish Need Apply). Built power through the Church, the saloon, ward politics, the police + fire houses, and labor.
ireland.arc: ARRIVAL (1800s): destitute, despised, parish + ward. CONVERGENCE (20th c.): the machine matures into real political power, unions, the Church's institutional weight, mid-century arrival into the mainstream (a Catholic in high office). FUTURE (21st c.+): post-ethnic, the 'Irish' identity now one thread among many in descendants who've intermarried across the other waves.
ireland.motivations: respectability + belonging against contempt; loyalty to parish + clan; political power as the route up.
ireland.trades: dockwork, domestic service, construction, police/fire, machine politics, the priesthood.
ireland.obstacles: nativist bigotry, anti-Catholicism, the stereotype of the drunken brawler.
ireland.braidAffinity: crosses ITALIAN (shared docks/construction, parish rivalry over the same Catholic turf), CHINESE (labor competition + shared nativist target), ASHKENAZI (ward politics vs the garment economy). Later tiers: the lines INTERMARRY + ally as the ethnic walls fall.

italian.history: Southern-Italian/Sicilian labor migration; padrone labor system, sojourner culture (earn + return), packed into Little Italys; faced lynching + 'not white enough' suspicion.
italian.arc: ARRIVAL (1800s): padrone labor, Little Italy, suspicion. CONVERGENCE (20th c.): into the building trades + business + entertainment, fighting the Mafia stereotype into mainstream respectability mid-century. FUTURE (21st c.+): assimilated, the village loyalty echoing in descendants spread across the other lines.
italian.motivations: family + village loyalty (la famiglia), distrust of the state, the dream of land/a business back home or here.
italian.trades: construction + excavation, masonry, produce + groceries, barbering, garment piecework, the trades.
italian.obstacles: the padrone's exploitation, anti-Italian violence, the Mafia stereotype.
italian.braidAffinity: crosses IRISH (same construction gangs + Catholic parishes, often as rivals), ASHKENAZI (the garment trade — Italian seamstresses, Jewish shops), CHINESE (street-market competition). Later tiers: intermarriage + business alliance as Little Italy dissolves into the suburbs.

ashkenazi_jewish.history: Fleeing Russian/Eastern-European pogroms + the Pale; arrived into the Lower East Side; built the garment industry, then union militancy (ILGWU), education, and upward mobility into the professions.
ashkenazi_jewish.arc: ARRIVAL (1800s): pogrom refugees, the needle trade, the tenement. CONVERGENCE (20th c.): education → the professions, finance, and the founding of entertainment (Hollywood, Tin Pan Alley); quotas falling mid-century. FUTURE (21st c.+): tech + science + culture at the highest reach, the shtetl an ancestral memory in widely-intermarried descendants.
ashkenazi_jewish.motivations: education + the book as escape; community self-help (landsmanshaftn); making it so the children rise.
ashkenazi_jewish.trades: garment + needle trades, peddling, small retail, then law/medicine/finance/entertainment/tech.
ashkenazi_jewish.obstacles: pogrom trauma, quotas + genteel antisemitism, sweatshop exploitation.
ashkenazi_jewish.braidAffinity: crosses ITALIAN (the garment shop floor — Jewish owners/cutters, Italian operators), IRISH (ward politics + landlord/tenant), CHINESE (Lower East Side / adjacent immigrant blocks), BAGHDAD (a fraught Jewish-world meeting across class/culture). Later tiers: alliance + intermarriage at the top of finance/culture.

chinese.history: Gold Rush + railroad labor, then the 1882 Exclusion Act — barred, bachelor societies, driven into Chinatowns; survived via laundries, restaurants, and tight clan/tong networks.
chinese.arc: ARRIVAL (1800s): railroad labor → Exclusion, bachelor Chinatowns. CONVERGENCE (20th c.): Exclusion repealed (1943), family reunification, the move into science/medicine/business through education. FUTURE (21st c.+): ascendant in tech + science + global trade, the exclusion era a remembered wound in a now-rooted, intermarried line.
chinese.motivations: send money home + endure exclusion; clan + tong loyalty; survival of a community legally denied a future.
chinese.trades: railroad + mining labor, laundries, restaurants, import/grocery, herbalists, then science/medicine/tech.
chinese.obstacles: the Exclusion Act + legal barring, violence + driving-out, the bachelor-society gender imbalance.
chinese.braidAffinity: crosses IRISH (railroad/mining labor competition + shared scapegoating), ITALIAN (street-vending + restaurant turf), ASHKENAZI (adjacent immigrant retail blocks). Later tiers: peers + partners in the professions + tech as exclusion gives way.

scandinavian.history: Norwegian/Swedish/Danish migration to the Upper Midwest; homesteading the prairie, Lutheran farm communities, later progressive + cooperative politics.
scandinavian.arc: ARRIVAL (1800s): breaking prairie sod, Lutheran farm towns. CONVERGENCE (20th c.): cooperative + progressive politics, into engineering/aerospace/design, the move from farm to city/profession. FUTURE (21st c.+): a quiet civic + engineering legacy, the prairie thrift echoing in descendants now woven into the urban + national lines.
scandinavian.motivations: land + self-sufficiency; Lutheran thrift + community; quiet civic virtue.
scandinavian.trades: homestead farming, logging, fishing, carpentry, cooperatives, eventually engineering/aerospace/design.
scandinavian.obstacles: brutal prairie winters + isolation, crop failure + debt, the slow grind of breaking sod.
scandinavian.braidAffinity: crosses BAVARIA (shared Midwest farm belt + Lutheran/brewing culture), and the others mostly at the later urban/national tiers (engineering, civic politics), not the immigrant ward.

bavaria.history: German (Bavarian) migration; brewing, skilled trades, the Turnverein + Vereine societies, strong in the Midwest + the brewing cities; faced WWI-era anti-German backlash.
bavaria.arc: ARRIVAL (1800s): brewing + skilled trades + Vereine societies. CONVERGENCE (20th c.): the WWI/WWII anti-German rupture (names anglicized, identity submerged) + Prohibition gutting brewing, then quiet reassimilation into industry + engineering. FUTURE (21st c.+): the craft + institution-building legacy carried forward, German identity long dissolved into the mainstream.
bavaria.motivations: craft + Gemütlichkeit + civic order; the guild-skilled pride; building durable institutions.
bavaria.trades: brewing, baking, butchery, skilled machining + tool-making, music + civic societies, then industry/engineering.
bavaria.obstacles: WWI/WWII anti-German sentiment (names anglicized, language suppressed), Prohibition gutting brewing.
bavaria.braidAffinity: crosses SCANDINAVIAN (Midwest farm/brewing belt), IRISH (saloon vs brewery, urban Catholic/Lutheran), and peer rivalry with established houses at later tiers. The anti-German era can sever or test old crossings.

baghdad.history: Baghdadi/Sephardic Jewish merchant diaspora — a trading network spanning Baghdad→Bombay→Shanghai→London (the Sassoon model); the one non-1885, earlier + globally-networked origin.
baghdad.arc: ARRIVAL (earlier, ~1800s, non-US-bound): a merchant house already spanning ports. CONVERGENCE (20th c.): banking + the multinational family firm through the age of empires + their fall. FUTURE (21st c.+): global finance + trade at the highest reach, a cosmopolitan house with no single homeland, woven into the other elite lines.
baghdad.motivations: the trade network + family firm across continents; cosmopolitan adaptability; honor of the merchant house.
baghdad.trades: long-distance trade (textiles, opium-era commerce, finance), banking, the multinational family firm.
baghdad.obstacles: outsider everywhere + at home nowhere, shifting empires + ports, the precarity of a stateless network.
baghdad.braidAffinity: crosses ASHKENAZI (Jewish-world ties across very different class/cultural registers — a genuine, fraught meeting), and established merchant/finance houses at the upper tiers; rarely the immigrant-ward poor.
